# REQ-QR-260005: Dual-View Switch Performance Targets and Large-Render Gate Timing

- Class: QR
- Status: Accepted
- Date: 2026-05-12
- Source decision: define measurable dual-view performance acceptance thresholds

## Requirement

Dual-view performance acceptance uses these targets (baseline desktop profile):

- <=150 nodes: p95 view-switch visual update <= 200ms.
- 151-400 nodes (reduced mode): p95 view-switch visual update <= 400ms.
- >400 nodes: warning gate blocks rendering until user continue; first paint after continue <= 1.5s.

## Rationale

Defined thresholds create objective acceptance criteria and avoid ambiguous performance regressions.

## Scope Notes

- In scope for this requirement: view-switch latency and gated first-paint timing.
- Out of scope for this requirement: deep backend query latency optimization unrelated to view-switch/render path.

## Verification

- Measurement checks validate p95 thresholds for <=150 and 151-400 ranges.
- Gate-path check validates continue confirmation and post-confirm first-paint timing.
- Regression check confirms threshold breaches are treated as quality-gate failures.

## Traceability

- Related ADRs: ADR-260032
- Related backlog items: BI-260033
